Back to top

Model adapters¶

Adapters abstract OpenAI and Anthropic APIs behind a common interface. Use create_adapter() to instantiate the correct adapter from a model name string.

create_adapter(model, api_key=None, **kwargs)[source]¶

Instantiate the appropriate ModelAdapter for model.

Provider is inferred from the model name prefix:

  • gpt-*, o1*, o3* → OpenAIAdapter

  • claude-* → AnthropicAdapter

Pass api_key to override the default environment-variable lookup. Any additional keyword arguments are forwarded to the adapter constructor (e.g. max_tokens for AnthropicAdapter).

Parameters:

  • model (str) – Model identifier string (e.g. "gpt-4o" or "claude-sonnet-4-20250514").

  • api_key (str | None) – Optional API key override.

  • **kwargs (object) – Extra keyword arguments forwarded to the adapter.

Returns:

A concrete ModelAdapter instance ready to call.

Raises:

ConfigurationError – If the provider cannot be inferred from model, or if the resolved adapter cannot find an API key.

Return type:

ModelAdapter

Base adapter¶

Abstract model adapter base class.

All provider-specific adapters inherit from ModelAdapter. The contract is intentionally minimal: receive a prompt (and optional system prompt), return the model’s text response as a plain string. This keeps the runner and judge decoupled from any provider SDK.

class ModelAdapter[source]¶

Bases: ABC

Provider-agnostic interface for sending prompts to an LLM.

Concrete subclasses must implement complete() and model_id. All network I/O must be async so the runner can fan out test cases concurrently without blocking.

abstract property model_id: str¶

The model identifier string used when calling the provider API.

Examples: "gpt-4o", "claude-sonnet-4-20250514".

abstractmethod async complete(prompt, system_prompt=None)[source]¶

Send a prompt to the model and return the text response.

Parameters:

  • prompt (str) – The user-turn message to send.

  • system_prompt (str | None) – Optional system / instruction prefix. When None the adapter’s provider default (if any) is used.

Returns:

The model’s text response. Never None — returns an empty string if the provider returns no content.

Raises:

ModelAdapterError – If the provider API call fails for any reason (auth error, rate limit, timeout, malformed response, etc.).

Return type:

str

Anthropic adapter¶

Anthropic model adapter.

Wraps the anthropic async client so the runner can call Claude models through the same ModelAdapter interface as every other provider.

Anthropic’s Messages API differs from OpenAI’s in two notable ways:

  • max_tokens is required — the adapter exposes it as a constructor parameter with a sensible default.

  • The system prompt is a top-level parameter, not a role: system message in the messages list.

API key resolution order:

  1. api_key constructor argument.

  2. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable (loaded from .env).

Raises ConfigurationError at construction time if no key is available.

class AnthropicAdapter(model, api_key=None, max_tokens=4096)[source]¶

Bases: ModelAdapter

Adapter for Anthropic Claude models.

Parameters:

  • model (str) – Anthropic model identifier (e.g. "claude-sonnet-4-20250514").

  • api_key (str | None) – Anthropic API key. When None the value of the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable is used.

  • max_tokens (int) – Maximum tokens to generate per response. Defaults to 4096.

Raises:

ConfigurationError – If no API key can be found.

property model_id: str¶

Anthropic model identifier passed at construction.

async complete(prompt, system_prompt=None)[source]¶

Call the Anthropic Messages API and return the text response.

Concatenates all text-type content blocks in the response. In practice Claude returns a single block, but the API allows multiple.

Parameters:

  • prompt (str) – The user-turn message.

  • system_prompt (str | None) – Optional system prompt passed as the top-level system parameter (not as a message role).

Returns:

The assistant’s text reply, or an empty string if no text blocks are present in the response.

Raises:

ModelAdapterError – Wraps any anthropic.APIError.

Return type:

str

OpenAI adapter¶

OpenAI model adapter.

Wraps the openai async client so the runner can call OpenAI chat models (GPT-4o, o1, o3, etc.) through the same ModelAdapter interface as every other provider.

API key resolution order:

  1. api_key constructor argument (useful in tests / programmatic use).

  2. OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable (loaded from .env via python-dotenv).

Raises ConfigurationError at construction time if no key is available, so misconfiguration is caught before any API call.

class OpenAIAdapter(model, api_key=None)[source]¶

Bases: ModelAdapter

Adapter for OpenAI chat-completion models.

Parameters:

  • model (str) – OpenAI model identifier (e.g. "gpt-4o", "o3-mini").

  • api_key (str | None) – OpenAI API key. When None the value of the OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable is used.

Raises:

ConfigurationError – If no API key can be found.

property model_id: str¶

OpenAI model identifier passed at construction.

async complete(prompt, system_prompt=None)[source]¶

Call the OpenAI chat completions API and return the text response.

Parameters:

  • prompt (str) – The user-turn message.

  • system_prompt (str | None) – Optional system message prepended to the conversation.

Returns:

The assistant’s text reply, or an empty string if the API returns None content.

Raises:

ModelAdapterError – Wraps any openai.APIError.

Return type:

str
